library.description: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3: النوافذ، الإدخال، الأحداث، الصوت، والرسم الأساسي.

kind.description: أنواع مجردة أو صريحة تحمل معنى دلالياً وتظهر في التواقيع والبنى.

identity.name: SDL_AudioStreamCallback
identity.titleArabic: A callback that fires when data passes through an SDL_AudioStream

summary.actualTranslationArabic: A callback that fires when data passes through an SDL_AudioStream
summary.actualMeaningArabic: يمثل هذا النوع نوع SDL الصوت الدفق Callback، أي القيمة أو المقبض أو المؤشر الذي تتعامل معه بقية الواجهة في هذا السياق.
summary.purposeArabic: يستخدم هذا النوع عندما تحتاج القيمة أو المقبض أو المؤشر الذي يمثله داخل بقية الواجهة.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا أنه يمثل نوع القيمة التي تتداولها بقية الواجهة بين الحقول والدوال.
summary.carriedValueArabic: يحمل هذا النوع القيمة الفعلية أو المقبض أو المؤشر الذي تمرره بقية الدوال والحقول في هذا المسار.
summary.sourceDescription: A callback that fires when data passes through an SDL_AudioStream .

signature.raw: typedef void (SDLCALL *SDL_AudioStreamCallback)(void *userdata, SDL_AudioStream *stream, int additional_amount, int total_amount);

remarks:
Apps can (optionally) register a callback with an audio stream that
is called when data is added with SDL_PutAudioStreamData , or
requested with SDL_GetAudioStreamData .

Two values are offered here: one is the amount of additional data
needed to satisfy the immediate request (which might be zero if the
stream already has enough data queued) and the other is the total amount
being requested. In a Get call triggering a Put callback, these values
can be different. In a Put call triggering a Get callback, these values
are always the same.

Byte counts might be slightly overestimated due to buffering or
resampling, and may change from call to call.

This callback is not required to do anything. Generally this is
useful for adding/reading data on demand, and the app will often put/get
data as appropriate, but the system goes on with the data currently
available to it if this callback does nothing.
details.threadSafety:
This callbacks may run from any thread, so if you need to protect
shared data, you should use SDL_LockAudioStream to serialize
access; this lock will be held before your callback is called, so your
callback does not need to manage the lock explicitly.
